rulings.Case Title: 1151 ALABAMA DEVELOPMENT GROUP, LLC, VS. FRANCISCA JIMENEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2021, line 1. DEFENDANT GUILLERMO MEDINA, FRANCISCA JIMENEZ's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. transferred to department 210 to be heard by the honorable Vedica Puri in August 31, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL V. CHOPKO ET AL VS. VASILIKI SERVETA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2021, line 3. DEFENDANT VASILIKI SERVETA's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting its position that reference to plaintiff as a "lessor" in the notice does not render the complaint, disclaiming any landlord-tenant" relationship, uncertain. Plaintiff shall also in good faith address the issues raised regarding UD-101. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
